mnemonics: Think 'BI-cycle' has TWO wheels, a BI-gram is a sequence of TWO words.
A BI-nocular sees TWO things, a BI-gram analyzes TWO consecutive words.
keyPoints: A bigram is a sequence of two adjacent elements (tokens), usually words.
Bigrams are fundamental for building n-gram language models to predict the next word.
They are used to capture simple local dependencies and word associations in text.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that bigrams are order-sensitive ('the cat' is different from 'cat the').
Assuming bigrams are only for words; they can also be characters or other tokens.
Over-relying on bigrams and missing longer-range context that trigrams or higher n-grams capture.
